name: Pillars Fund
mission: Pillars Fund amplifies the leadership, narratives, and talents of Muslims in the United States to advance opportunity and justice for all.
theoryOfChange: Pillars Fund explicitly states its mission to "amplify the leadership, narratives, and talents of Muslims in the United States to advance opportunity and justice for all." They are focused on "narrative and cultural power — tapping into a rich inheritance of Muslim storytelling to transform society." This approach is further detailed as a "long-term narrative and culture change strategy for and with the American Muslim community." Their work involves supporting nonprofit organizations and creative leaders who are building narrative and cultural power for social change, and they have initiatives like the Pillars Artist Fellowship to empower creatives to tell their own stories. This demonstrates a clear, documented program strategy linking their activities to their overarching goals and desired impact.
zakatClaimEvidence: Pillars Fund is described as a grant-making organization and Muslim community foundation focused on funding secular non-profit programs supporting American Muslims. There is no explicit mention on their website or in the provided search results that they accept zakat donations from individuals or have dedicated zakat funds. (Source: https://www.pillarsfund.org)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

components.evidence: Muslim-focused organization (+2); Islamic identity (+1); Asnaf: fi_sabilillah (+5)
components.improvement_suggestion: Add a dedicated zakat page or clearly state whether donations are zakat-eligible.
components.name: Muslim Donor Fit

alignment.rationale: Alignment 25/50: MEDIUM Muslim donor fit, religious cultural cause area

components.evidence: No beneficiary data available for cost-per-beneficiary scoring; moderate program ratio (≥65%)
components.improvement_suggestion: Publish beneficiary counts consistently so cost-effectiveness can be assessed.
components.name: Cost Per Beneficiary

components.improvement_suggestion: If reserves are above policy target, document why and publish triggers for planned deployment.
components.name: Financial Health

risks.description: No outcome measurement or metrics tracking

score_details.score_summary: Pillars Fund shows strong impact and moderate alignment (minor risk deductions applied).

zakat.claim_evidence: Pillars Fund is described as a grant-making organization and Muslim community foundation focused on funding secular non-profit programs supporting American Muslims. There is no explicit mention on their website or in the provided search results that they accept zakat donations from individuals or have dedicated zakat funds. (Source: https://www.pillarsfund.org)

all_citations.claim: The organization reported a total revenue of $2,398,957 and a program expense ratio of 74.3%.
Financial data indicates a working capital of 20.7 months and a fundraising efficiency of $0.31 per $1 raised.
Pillars Fund strategically supports nonprofit organizations that amplify the talents and leadership of American Muslims.
The organization supports fellows through its MNC cohort and storytelling initiatives.
The fund tracks outcomes through multimedia contributions and artist fellowships.
Donations are accepted as general charitable giving (sadaqah).

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: Pillars Fund earns a 64/100 score, reflecting a high impact in capacity building and cultural representation, balanced by a medium alignment score due to its specific focus on religious and cultural advocacy.
areas_for_improvement: Fundraising efficiency could be optimized, currently costing $0.31 to raise every $1
Limited third-party impact evaluations available for long-term cultural outcomes

dimension_explanations.alignment: Pillars Fund offers a medium donor fit for those seeking to support American Muslim civic and cultural infrastructure [3]. While it maintains a strong track record in its niche, the cause urgency is categorized as religious and cultural advocacy, and the organization specifically accepts sadaqah rather than zakat [6].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization demonstrates high effectiveness in capacity building, reporting a total revenue of $2,398,957 and maintaining a healthy 74.3% program expense ratio [1][2]. Its initiatives have successfully supported fellows in MNC cohorts and facilitated significant multimedia contributions to the cultural sector [4][5].

baseline_narrative.headline: Pillars Fund empowers American Muslims by strategically investing in storytelling and leadership to reshape cultural narratives.
strengths: Strong financial stability with 20.7 months of working capital available to sustain operations
High programmatic focus with a 74.3% program expense ratio dedicated to its mission
Robust support for Muslim creatives through specialized fellowships and multimedia contributions
baseline_narrative.summary: Pillars Fund is a non-profit organization dedicated to strengthening relations between American Muslims and the broader public by amplifying Muslim talents and leadership [3]. The organization operates key initiatives such as the Pillars Artist Fellowship and Storytelling Grants to support creators and nonprofit leaders [4][5]. Contributions to the fund are classified as sadaqah-eligible, providing a pathway for general charitable giving toward cultural and civic empowerment [6].
